issues.body: SnapshotSync clones only the source Replica's State and applies it to the receiver. If a dot was added and then removed before the snapshot, State retains the dot only in the causal context; it no longer contains the quad association, and Replica::apply records provenance only for dots present in delta.store(). The receiver therefore cannot know that the historical dot belonged to the original quad and accepts a later delta asserting it under a different quad. This contradicts origin.rs's claim that reuse is rejected for the full lifetime of every observed dot and leaves the round-2 defect reachable through the modeled CRDT-JOURNAL-2 snapshot recovery path.
issues.fix_hint: Make snapshot import/export carry durable dot-to-quad provenance (or equivalent validation metadata) and merge it fail-closed, rather than passing State alone. Add a regression that creates add(q1,d), removes d, snapshot-syncs a fresh replica, then asserts that forged add(q2,d) is rejected with both state and provenance unchanged. Also exercise this through generated SnapshotSync schedules so catch-up cannot silently lose the validation journal.
issues.title: Snapshot catch-up drops the provenance required by CRDT-WIRE-4

summary: Round 3 fixes CRDT-WIRE-4 rejection for replicas that previously received the original add: Replica now retains dot-to-quad provenance after the live store entry is removed, and the directed regression exercises that path. However, the advertised snapshot/catch-up path transfers only State. State contains the tombstoned dot in its causal context but not its historical quad association, so a replica initialized through SnapshotSync cannot reconstruct dot_provenance and will accept a later forged add that reuses the tombstoned dot under another quad. The full-lifetime CRDT-WIRE-4 claim therefore remains false across a supported recovery path. Progress is stagnant because the same major historical-reuse defect remains at the same severity, although its direct-envelope case is now fixed.
